title: Manettia congesta
taxonomy: Manettia congesta is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the Americas. The plant is classified in the genus Manettia, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the Liatris or Blazing Star. Manettia congesta is further classified as a member of the order Gentianales, and the class Magnoliopsida. The classification of Manettia congesta reflects its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants.
uses: Manettia congesta, also known as the Panama flower, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to their anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and infections. In addition, Manettia congesta is used to treat digestive issues such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Manettia congesta for medicinal purposes.

parts: Manettia congesta, also known as scarlet powderflower or flame flower, is a plant species native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. intro: Manettia congesta, also known as Jamaica dogwood or Yellow Dogwood, is a shrub or small tree native to Central and South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may aid in relieving pain and reducing fever. Bioactive constituents of Manettia congesta include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, such as berberine, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including anxiety, insomnia, and muscle pain.
